steps.title: Spot the contrast between assumption and reality
steps.content: The passage sets up a mismatch: the name “underground” creates the assumption of secrecy, but the next clause reveals his views “were quite well known.” The blank must signal this reversal.

steps.title: Apply the contradiction signal
steps.content:
”On the contrary” directly marks the contradiction — it tells us the second clause contradicts the assumption created by the word “underground.” The other three either add related information (Additionally), introduce an instance (For example), or show causation (As a result) — none of which signal the contradiction here.

That confirms D is correct.
